query_basis: Programs table rows with verified state program evidence.
Waiver-like program rows or fallback to verified program rows when the state stores waiver entry in the general spine.
Verified early-intervention or Part C agency rows.
Verified regional education or district pages with special-education routing.

samples.sample_name: Oregon Department of Education Special Education

query_basis: Reviewed live ODE School Directory page plus the current official Combined Directory PDF.

samples.evidence_snippet: The live page says the Oregon School Directory is an online resource for all public schools and districts and explicitly says the PDF index allows users to search by county.
The public-schools section states Oregon education service districts and school districts are listed alphabetically by county.
The example district block for Baker SD 5J preserves institution ID, phone, fax, street address, website, and Superintendent Casey Hallgarth.
The PDF index includes schools, districts, cities, and counties.

query_basis: Verified programs with VR or Pre-ETS context.
Reviewed first-party Disability Rights Oregon artifact preserves Oregon-specific disability-related legal help identity, contact information, and first-party domain evidence.

samples.evidence_snippet: Disability Rights Oregon helps people with disabilities with their disability-related legal issues in Oregon, and the reviewed first-party page preserves welcome@droregon.org plus the Portland office address.

query_basis: Reviewed first-party Oregon legal-aid host with statewide help scope and office structure.

samples.evidence_snippet: Oregon Law Center (OLC) provides free legal help to people struggling to make ends meet and preserves statewide hotline and office help structure.

query_basis: Verified ABLE program pages from the program spine.
Reviewed the live Oregon ODHS office-finder root, bounded county/city query probes, statewide robots/search surfaces, and the existing county-office source split on disk.
blocker_evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-23 one bounded official Oregon county-local replacement lane on the live ODHS office-finder stack. The old `https://dhhs.oregon.gov/locations` host still fails DNS, and the current successor root `https://www.oregon.gov/odhs/pages/office-finder.aspx` is confirmed live on the official Oregon host. But the live surface still fails closed for county-grade routing: the page returns an ASP.NET SharePoint shell titled `Find an Office`, loads Leaflet and marker-cluster libraries, and preserves only generic help text such as `Look up ODHS offices near you and get contact information and directions. Choose the kind of service you need and find an office close to you.` Bounded query-string probes like `?county=Baker` and `?city=Salem` return the same generic page, the static HTML preserves no county list, office rows, or public result payload, `robots.txt` only confirms statewide SharePoint exclusions, the obvious sitemap and search surfaces return 404, and no public endpoint or export contract is exposed in the reviewed page source. A bounded DB check still shows Oregon county-office rows split between 61 DOI-backed planning rows and only 3 dead `dhhs.oregon.gov/locations` rows. Oregon therefore remains blocked because the live successor lane is real but still only a public app shell with no county-grade office extract.

samples.sample_name: Live ODHS Office Finder successor root
samples.evidence_snippet: Find an Office. Look up ODHS offices near you and get contact information and directions. Choose the kind of service you need and find an office close to you.

samples.sample_name: Bounded county query returns same generic shell
samples.evidence_snippet: The county query probe returns the same generic Find an Office shell rather than county-specific office rows.

samples.sample_name: Bounded city query returns same generic shell
samples.evidence_snippet: The city query probe returns the same generic Find an Office shell rather than a public office result payload.

samples.sample_name: Official statewide robots and search surfaces do not expose a county lane
samples.evidence_snippet: The statewide robots file only confirms SharePoint exclusions, while the obvious search result URLs reviewed in this lane return 404 and add no county-office contract.
